video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out after drone interception in UAE
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Saturday in Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates following an interception of a drone, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Iraq is caught in the crossfire of the Iran war, with attacks by both sides on its soil - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Iraq is getting caught in the crossfire of the Iran war as the only country facing strikes from both sides, and that threatens to drag the nation that has so far avoided two years of regional turmoil into a full-blown crisis.

video:video.video:title: Dust storm blows over Gaza as people are urged to protect themselves
video:video.video:description: First responders told Palestinians to stay home, especially those with respiratory illness, as a cloud of orange-tinted, thick dust spread across the enclave.

video:video.video:title: Nobel laureate Machado rallies with Venezuelan diaspora in Chile, says there is a plan to return
video:video.video:description: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado on Thursday said millions who left their country can return and help rebuild it. (AP video shot by Mauricio Cuevas)

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of US strikes on Kharg Island, Iran
video:video.video:description: Video from the U.S. Central Command showed U.S. forces destroying targets on the ground, which it claims were in Kharg Island, Iran.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds at least 15, with peace talks stalled
video:video.video:description: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds at least 15, with peace talks stalled

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler 'not thinking about' potential Oscars history
video:video.video:description: Ryan Coogler shares what he is focused on heading into Oscar Sunday. His film "Sinners" has an historic 16 nominations.

video:video.video:title: 'KPop Demon Hunters' star "thrilled" for sequel
video:video.video:description: "KPop Demon Hunters" star Arden Cho hints that the newly-announced sequel to the Oscar-nominated animated feature may arrive sooner than we think.

video:video.video:title: Brandi Carlile on what Americana means to her
video:video.video:description: Brandi Carlile shares how she defines Americana. The singer-songwriter announced the expansion of “The Human Tour" Friday.

video:video.video:title: Homes damaged by falling debris after Pakistan says it intercepted drones launched by Taliban
video:video.video:description: Drone attacks on Pakistan intercepted by the country's military on Friday injured two children in the city of Quetta and damaged homes.

video:video.video:title: Iran warns UAE about possible attack on U.S military 'hideouts', urges citizens to evacuate
video:video.video:description: Iran's joint military command threatened to attack cities in the UAE, home to Dubai and one of the world's busiest airports, saying the U.S. used “ports, docks and hideouts” there to launch strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, without providing evidence.

video:video.video:title: Trump departs Trump International Golf Club in West Palm Beach
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump departs his golf club in West Palm Beach, as he spends this weekend at Mar-a-Lago.

video:video.video:title: Chicago River turns green for St. Patrick's day
video:video.video:description: The Chicago River was glowing a bright emerald green on Saturday as the city kicks off its St. Patrick’s Day celebrations, a tradition started in 1962 by the Chicago Journeymen Plumbers Local 130.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 13th: U.S. embassy struck by missile in Baghdad; U.S. hits military sites on Iran's Kharg Island; American flag raised at U.S. embassy in Venezuela; Chicago River turns green for St.

video:video.video:title: Comic connoisseurs and cosplayers gather in Prague for sixth annual Comic Con
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans gathered at the sixth annual Comic-Con Prague, a three-day pop culture event with interactive workshops, a gaming zone, multiple lectures, and insights into upcoming films.

video:video.video:title: Police fire tear gas during protest against labor reform in Ecuador
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of workers, students, and teachers protested Friday in Ecuador’s capital Quito after the government extended daily work shift from eight to 10 hours, with a weekly cap of 40 hours.

video:video.video:title: US raises flag at embassy in Caracas after seven-year diplomatic freeze ends
video:video.video:description: The United States flag was raised again at the U.S. Embassy in Caracas on Saturday, seven years after it was lowered when Washington and Caracas cut diplomatic relations in 2019.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area
video:video.video:description: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area

video:video.video:title: AP Exclusive: Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan talks diplomatic efforts as regional war rages
video:video.video:description: Turkey’s foreign minister Hakan Fidan said Saturday that there is no serious initiative to resume negotiations between the United States and Iran but that he believes Iran is open to back-channel talks.

video:video.video:title: Villagers live in fear after drones attack nearby refinery in Irbil, Iraq
video:video.video:description: Locals in a village in Irbil were living in fear on Friday, after a drone strike hit the nearby Lanaz refinery in the Iraqi city.

video:video.video:title: South Korea and US hold joint exercises as part of annual military drills
video:video.video:description: The South Korean and U.S. militaries on Saturday held a joint training exercise at a river in Yeoncheon, north of South Korea's capital, as part of their annual Freedom Shield combined exercise.

video:video.video:title: Atlanta celebrates St. Patrick's day with a parade
video:video.video:description: Over 2,000 people participated in a St. Patrick’s Day parade in Atlanta on Saturday. The parade was part of boisterous celebrations being held in cities across the US.

video:video.video:title: Millions in Vietnam voting for a new legislature in general election
video:video.video:description: Vietnam's Communist Party General Secretary To Lam was among those casting their ballot - Nearly 79 million voters across the country are eligible to pick 500 representatives from 864 candidates to the rubber-stamp National Assembly.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim observes test of rocket launch systems with his daughter
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, accompanied by his teenage daughter, observed a live-fire test of multiple rocket launch systems, state media reported Sunday, a likely response to ongoing U.S.

video:video.video:title: Fatalities after passenger bus carrying Indian pilgrims slips off mountain road in Nepal
video:video.video:description: The bus was returning after the pilgrims visited the revered Manakamana Temple, a popular Hindu temple where devotees believe a goddess will grant them their wish if they visit the shrine.

video:video.video:title: Kazakhstanis vote in referendum on new constitution
video:video.video:description: The referendum puts to a vote a new constitution that would strengthen President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev’s grip on power in Central Asia’s largest country.

video:video.video:title: Hamas says officers killed in Israeli strike on police vehicle on south-north Salah al-Din route in Gaza
video:video.video:description: At least 12 Palestinians, including two boys, a pregnant woman and eight police officers, were killed Sunday by Israeli airstrikes in the war-torn Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Residents and emergency workers clear strike damage in Tehran's Javadieh neighborhood
video:video.video:description: The strike hit a police station in the area of Javadieh and some surrounding buildings were damaged.

video:video.video:title: Voters in France share their thoughts as the country takes to the polls for municipal elections
video:video.video:description: Sunday is the first round of municipal elections to choose leadership teams across France, from small villages to the biggest cities - a test for political parties' electoral machines before next year's presidential election for the successor to President Emmanuel Macron.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and strong winds batter tents sheltering displaced along Beirut’s waterfront
video:video.video:description: Displaced families in Lebanon’s capital scrambled to secure plastic sheets and wooden support as the downpour soaked their makeshift tents. (AP video by Fadi Tawil)

video:video.video:title: Israeli group behind mystery flights transferring Palestinians out of Gaza
video:video.video:description: An AP investigation has found that an Israeli group whose founder strongly supported U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to resettle Palestinians from Gaza is behind several flights that transferred hundreds of people out of the war-torn enclave.

video:video.video:title: One family's journey from slavery to the White House
video:video.video:description: Presidents come and go from the White House every four or eight years, but the Ficklin family was a constant presence there for nearly eight decades, serving 13 presidents from FDR to Barack Obama.

video:video.video:title: Israeli group discreetly organized mystery flights evacuating Palestinians from Gaza - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: The plane carrying about 150 Palestinians from Gaza came as a surprise to everyone on the ground when it landed in South Africa in November. It wasn't the only one.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held after Israeli soldiers fire on family car in occupied West Bank, killing 4
video:video.video:description: The official Palestinian news agency said the family was shot late Saturday after going out to buy new clothes for Eid al-Fitr, the holiday marking the end of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan this week.

video:video.video:title: Cleanup operations take place in Beirut after Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Israel ramped up its strikes on its northern neighbor after the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah fired several rockets into the country following the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader - Israeli strikes have killed 826 people in Lebanon, including 106 children and 65 women, since the conflict between

video:video.video:title: 'Black rain' creates health, environmental risks as toxic smoke billows from bombed Iran oil depots
video:video.video:description: Airstrikes on Iran's oil depots are sending up billowing clouds of toxic smoke that health and environmental officials warn pose serious health threats to anyone who breathes or comes into contact with it.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike kills 4 in Gaza, including 2 children and a pregnant woman, according to hospital officials
video:video.video:description: The strike hit a house in Nuseirat, an urban refugee camp in central Gaza, killing a couple in their 30s and their 10-year son, according to the nearby Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital.

video:video.video:title: Elections in the Republic of Congo that could extend incumbent president's 42-year rule
video:video.video:description: Three million people are registered to vote in the election, which has been marked by a lethargic mood among young people who expect incumbent leader Sassou N’Guesso to win again, and a call for a boycott by opposition parties.

video:video.video:title: Israel PM orders coffee and dispels rumors of his death in online post
video:video.video:description: The video was posted on Benjamin Netanyahu's Facebook page, responding to viral posts claiming he'd been killed, which were followed by posts speculating that footage of him had been AI edited because he looked to have six fingers.

video:video.video:title: North Koreans take part in parliament election, seven years since last vote
video:video.video:description: The election is for the North's parliament, officially called the Supreme People's Assembly, which has more than 600 deputies from all over the country.

video:video.video:title: Orbán and challenger Magyar summon rival rallies in show of strength before Hungary's election
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of supporters gathered in Budapest on Sunday in rival rallies to show their backing for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and his challenger Péter Magyar in the upcoming elections.

video:video.video:title: Powerful storm chain brings blizzard conditions to Wisconsin
video:video.video:description: Warnings of hazardous road conditions were issued across Minnesota, Michigan and Wisconsin, where transportation officials warned of worsening conditions Sunday with low visibility and snow-covered roadways.

video:video.video:title: Rain brings landslides, rescues, and collapsed roads on Maui
video:video.video:description: Flash flooding has been a major problem in recent days in places like Maui, Molokai and the Big Island, where rain had been falling from 1 to 2 inches (2.5 to 5 centimeters) an hour overnight, according to the Hawaii Emergency Management Agency.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 15: President Trump appeals to other countries in sending warships to the Strait of Hormuz; Heavy snow in U.S. midwest as forecasters predict storms eastward; Boston holds annual St.

video:video.video:title: 'Heated Rivalry' star Hudson Williams arrives at Oscars.
video:video.video:description: “Heated Rivalry” star Hudson Williams arrives at the 2026 Oscars.

video:video.video:title: 'One Battle After Another' star Chase Infiniti arrives at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: “One Battle After Another” star Chase Infiniti arrives at the 2026 Oscars. The film has 13 nominations.

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem wears ‘No to war’ pin at Oscars
video:video.video:description: Javier Bardem wore a “No a la guerra” (“No to war”) pin and a another supporting Palestine on the red carpet at the 98th Academy Awards.

video:video.video:title: Teyana Taylor arrives at Oscars
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor arrives on the Oscars red carpet. She is nominated for best supporting actress for her role in “One Battle After Another.”

video:video.video:title: Fans gather for Wagner Moura look-alike contest as Brazil awaits Oscars
video:video.video:description: Fans gathered on Sunday for a look-alike contest honoring actor Wagner Moura, as excitement builds across the country ahead of the Academy Awards ceremony later that evening.

video:video.video:title: Best actress nominee Jessie Buckley arrives at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Best actress nominee Jessie Buckley arrives at the 98th Academy Awards. She is nominated for her role in “Hamnet.”

video:video.video:title: Joel Edgerton talks global issues on Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: On the red carpet of the Academy Awards, actor Joel Edgerton touches upon attending the event while "conflicts all over the world that are very complicated" are raging.

video:video.video:title: Polls close in Republic of Congo election as incumbent leader seeks fifth term
video:video.video:description: Polls close in Republic of Congo election as incumbent leader seeks fifth term

video:video.video:title: Fire and smoke seen as Israel strikes southern Beirut suburb
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said early Monday it struck more Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut. Moments before the announcement, a powerful strike hit Beirut’s southern suburbs.

video:video.video:title: Villanova players react to their March Madness Selection Sunday draw
video:video.video:description: The Selection Show for the highly anticipated NCAA single-elimination tournament features bluebloods, conference champs, Cinderellas and everything in between.
video:video.video:tag: NCAA Men's Division I Basketball Championship

video:video.video:title: AP reporters add context to Trump's reasons for attacking Iran
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration has been trying to explain why it struck Iran and killed its supreme leader, and the shifting reasons range from Iran’s nuclear program to its ballistic missiles capabilities.

video:video.video:title: Jessie Buckley, Timothée Chalamet pose at Oscars
video:video.video:description: Stars including Jessie Buckley, Timothée Chalamet and Leonardo DiCaprio pose for photographs ahead of the 98th Academy Awards. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Nordic and Canadian leaders express full support for Greenland and Denmark
video:video.video:description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Nordic leaders on Sunday expressed full support of Greenland and Denmark’s right for “self-determination”.

video:video.video:title: Best actor nominee Timothée Chalamet arrives at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Best actor nominee Timothée Chalamet arrives at the 98th Academy Awards. He is nominated for his role in “Marty Supreme.”

video:video.video:title: Star of ‘The Voice of Hind Rajab’ wears Artists4Ceasefire pin on Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Saja Kilani, a star of the docudrama “The Voice of Hind Rajab,” wears an Artists4Ceasefire pin on the Oscars red carpet.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti, EJAE, more, rock Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Stars including Chase Infiniti, AJAE, Renate Reinsve and Rose Byrne pose for photographs ahead of the 98th Academy Awards. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Protesters stop traffic outside Oscars
video:video.video:description: Police detained at least one protester on Sunday, part of a group blocking traffic near the Oscars. Protesters wearing shirts saying, “Stop child trafficking” huddled in the middle of the road a few blocks from the Oscars.

video:video.video:title: Star of 'KPop Demon Hunters' proud of Korean American roots
video:video.video:description: EJAE, who provides the singing voice of Rumi in “KPop Demon Hunters,” said she was “proud” to return to her roots as a Korean American woman during her Academy Awards performance of the best original song winner “Golden.”

video:video.video:title: Trump says that he's asked 'about 7' countries to join coalition to police Iran's Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has “demanded” that about seven countries heavily reliant on Middle East oil join a coalition to police the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: 'Sinners'' Ludwig Göransson on his father and his love for the blues
video:video.video:description: Ludwig Göransson won the Oscar for original score for “Sinners,” winning his third Academy Award. He spoke about his father and his love for the blues.

video:video.video:title: Jessie Buckley won her first Oscar, and revealed her daughter got her first tooth
video:video.video:description: Jessie Buckley took home an Oscar for Best Actress at Sunday's Academy Awards for her role in "Hamnet."

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler praises teachers backstage at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: After winning the original screenplay Oscar for “Sinners," Ryan Coogler credited a college English professor for his entry to Hollywood. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan after Oscar win: 'Dream Big. Be Kind'
video:video.video:description: Michael B. Jordan won best actor at the 98th Academy Awards, for playing identical twins Smoke and Stack in "Sinners." (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw becomes first woman to win Oscar for cinematography
video:video.video:description: Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman to win an Academy Award for best cinematography. She was recognized for her work on Ryan Coogler’s “Sinners.”

video:video.video:title: Mexico City massive soccer class sets Guinness record ahead of 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: Mexico City set a world record for the largest soccer class ever on Sunday as 9,500 participants filled the capital’s Zocalo square as the country prepares to host the opening match of the World Cup in June.

video:video.video:title: Michael B Jordan on Oscars Red Carpet
video:video.video:description: Michael B. Jordan, best actor nominee, arrives on the red carpet at the 98th Academy Awards. He is nominated for his role as twins in “Sinners.”

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler uplifts educators after Oscar win
video:video.video:description: Oscar winner Ryan Coogler uplifts educators who may incorporate "Sinners" into classes: "I think they'll know how to do it best."

video:video.video:title: Joel Edgerton on conflicts around the world
video:video.video:description: Actor Joel Edgerton talks global issues on Oscars red carpet.

video:video.video:title: 'One Battle After Another' triumphs at Oscars, 'Sinners' makes history
video:video.video:description: Paul Thomas Anderson’s “One Battle After Another” was crowned best picture at the 98th Academy Awards, with "Sinners" star Michael B.

video:video.video:title: stars show support at Jordan Chiles' final UCLA home meet
video:video.video:description: Keke Palmer, Olandria Carthen, Chloe Bailey, Winnie Harlow, Normani and Lexie Brown show their support at Olympic gymnast Jordan Chiles' final UCLA home meet.

video:video.video:title: “Death Fest” in Bangkok promotes meaningful living and best end-of-life experiences
video:video.video:description: How do you want to die? At the Death Fest fair in Thailand, visitors are encouraged to plan for end of life. (AP video shot by: Ed Peerayot)

video:video.video:title: Crew members rescued from stricken Thai cargo ship return to Bangkok
video:video.video:description: Rescued crew members from a Thai cargo ship that was struck and set ablaze near the Strait of Hormuz have arrived in Bangkok from Oman.
